name: Mitochondrial delivery and transplantation bootstrap pass for June 3, 2026

scope.question: What is the current human-relevant evidence landscape for Mitochondrial Delivery and Transplantation, and what belongs in the first public baseline?

summary: The staged baseline assigns low-confidence animal_signal. Direct aging-relevant support is anchored to aged-animal mitochondrial delivery: young-mouse mitochondria improved aged-mouse cognitive and motor performance while shifting ATP and ROS, hippocampal mitochondrial transfusion up-regulated SDHB in aged mice, and young-rat mitochondria reduced prefrontal-cortex oxidative-stress and apoptosis markers in stressed aged rats. Human relevance is encouraging but bounded: ex vivo elderly-human CD4+ T-cell mito-transfer improved activation and senescence/exhaustion marker patterns, the pediatric cardiac pilot reported better ECMO separation after acute ischemia-reperfusion injury, and the acute-stroke phase 1 report found no significant adverse events in four treated subjects. None of the human evidence establishes treated-human aging efficacy.
notes: The top bootstrap queue item was selected from research/backlog/track-priority.v1.json and research/state/coverage-status.v1.json.
The public question was scoped to one mitochondrial_dysfunction track: mitochondrial-delivery-and-transplantation.
The direct stage anchor is animal evidence because no treated-human aging trial or older-adult aging-relevant efficacy result was identified.
The pediatric cardiac and acute-stroke human records were treated as acute disease translation and safety/feasibility context, not as aging efficacy evidence.
The elderly-human CD4+ T-cell result was kept at ex vivo support because it is not a treated-human intervention.
Registry records for NCT02851758 and NCT04998357 were staged as trial-watch context and source records, with status as of June 3, 2026.
next_actions: Run source-fidelity review for the staged PubMed and ClinicalTrials.gov records.
Run interpretation/forecast review with special attention to whether ex vivo elderly-human cells or acute human ischemia trials should remain below human_biomarker_signal for aging.
During review, check overlap boundaries with mitochondrial biogenesis/dynamics, selective autophagy/mitophagy, cellular senescence, and immune aging tracks.
If approved and published, inspect the mitochondrial dysfunction hallmark page and the mitochondrial delivery and transplantation track page for evidence-ladder coherence.
